articles.title: Gold Holds Decline as US Strikes on Iran Magnify Inflation Risks
articles.summary: Gold held a decline as a second day of US strikes against Iran drove energy prices higher and intensified concerns around inflation.

articles.title: Oil Rises as US Hits Iran, Stocks Lose Momentum: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: Oil climbed and government bonds fell after the US and Iran launched a fresh round of attacks, escalating tensions in the Middle East. Gold and silver declined.

articles.title: Hormuz Traffic Grinds to Near Halt After US, Iran Strike
articles.summary: The US military struck Iran for the second straight day, an escalation of violence that threatens efforts to reach a permanent peace deal. Bloomberg's Abeer Abu Omar reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Japan’s Five-Year Bond Sale Sees Decent Demand on Higher Yields
articles.summary: Demand at Japan’s five-year bond sale on Thursday was broadly in line with recent auctions, showing that elevated yields are attracting some buyers.

articles.title: Goldman Says Hormuz Flare-Up May Delay Recovery in Oil Supplies
articles.summary: A recovery in Middle Eastern oil supplies could be set back if renewed tensions disrupt shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, Goldman Sachs Group Inc. said.

articles.title: China Asks Bankers to Avoid Underwriting Short-Term LGFV Bonds
articles.summary: China is steering municipal borrowers away from issuing short-term bonds in the domestic market, according to people familiar with the matter, in its latest bid to curb fundraising by weaker issuers.
